pullRequest.title: Remove pytest-runner and deprecated test setup (fixes #443)
pullRequest.body:
## PR Title

```
Remove pytest-runner and deprecated test setup (fixes #443)
```

---

## PR Description

### Summary

This PR removes the use of `pytest-runner` and related deprecated setuptools options from the project. These changes resolve [[#443](https://github.com/MechanicalSoup/MechanicalSoup/issues/443)](https://github.com/MechanicalSoup/MechanicalSoup/issues/443) and bring the package in line with modern Python packaging and testing practices.

---

### Changes

* **Removed `pytest-runner` from `setup_requires` in `setup.py`.**
* **Removed `tests_require` from `setup.py`.**
* Test dependencies are now intended to be installed via `pip install -r tests/requirements.txt`.
* Updated documentation and README installation/testing instructions:

  * Replaced any `python setup.py install` with `pip install .`.
  * Replaced any `python setup.py test` with `pytest` (after installing test requirements).
  * Added or clarified steps to install test/dev dependencies and run tests.
  * Added optional instructions for running tests with `tox` for multi-environment support.

---

### Rationale

* `pytest-runner`, `setup_requires`, and `tests_require` are deprecated ([[see setuptools issue #1684](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/1684)](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/1684)).
* `pip install .` and explicit test requirement files are now the recommended practice for Python projects.
* Direct invocation of `pytest` is more robust and standard for running tests.
* These changes remove deprecation warnings and improve compatibility with modern Python tooling.

---

### Testing

* Fresh environments can now install and test with:

  ```sh
  pip install -r requirements.txt
  pip install -r tests/requirements.txt
  pytest
  ```

* All CI workflows have been reviewed to ensure compatibility with these changes.

---

### References

* [[pytest-runner deprecation notice](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-runner#deprecation-notice)](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-runner#deprecation-notice)
* [[setuptools issue #1684](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/1684)](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/1684)

---

pullRequest.title: test_stateful_browser.py: fix unclosed textarea element
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #450.

The HTML parser in libxml2 2.14 is more strict about closing textarea elements.

Thanks to @doko42 for reporting this issue.

pullRequest.title: feat(browser.py): add support for buttons with `formaction` attr
pullRequest.body:
# What
* Updates `browser.get_request_kwargs` to choose the `formaction` attribute in buttons nested forms as action URL instead of  the `action` attribute in the `form` tag.
* Updates `form.choose_submit` to use [bs4.element.Tag.decompose](https://www.crummy.com/software/BeautifulSoup/bs4/doc/#decompose), replacing the element with `<None></None>`, as opposed to deleting the `name` attribute, which would meet the goal of "only sending one submit element" mentioned in `Form.choose_submit`'s docstring.

# Why
* This is the correct behavior, [as specified in the w3/MDN docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#formaction).
* Not removing unchosen submit elements means we will always choose the first submit element in strict order.

# Caveats
Once a tag is `decompose`d, it's gone for good. I'm assuming this is correct behavior given the content of the docstring for `Form.choose_submit`.

Fixes #456
